name: PGW-Conformal: perturbation-graph-weighted conformal — falsification test
one_line: Does an EXTERNAL biology-graph distance-to-training-support normalizer give better conditional coverage under perturbation-distribution-shift than (a) vanilla split conformal and (b) leverage-weighted conformal (hat-matrix), at matched marginal coverage?

prior_art_acknowledged.umbrella_idea_occupied: arXiv:2603.02204 (Mar 2026) — perturbation-structured selective conformal (causal-descendant axis). We do NOT claim 'first perturbation-native conformal'.
prior_art_acknowledged.nearest_published_method: Leverage-Weighted Conformal Prediction (LWCP), Fadnavis arXiv:2602.12693 — normalizer = hat-matrix leverage in predictor design space. THE decisive baseline.
other: Tibshirani et al. 2019 arXiv:1904.06019 weighted CP
CF-GNN Huang et al. NeurIPS 2023 arXiv:2305.14535 (transductive, GNN-only)

method.base_residual: |y - yhat| from committed HistGBR base predictor on leakage-safe features
method.PGW_normalizer: sigma(d(p)) where d(p)=graph distance from perturbation gene p to the TRAINING-support gene set in STRING (softmin over support); sigma = isotonic regression of |residual| on d, floored at sigma_min
method.graph: STRING human, combined_score>=700, restricted to perturbed genes

protocol.shift: leave-perturbation-CLUSTER-out (LOCO): Louvain communities on STRING; hold out whole communities so test genes are graph-distant from calibration
protocol.primary_endpoint: conditional-coverage gap g = max over distance-strata |empirical_coverage - 0.90|, at MATCHED marginal coverage ~0.90
protocol.comparison: paired across folds: PGW vs vanilla, and PGW vs LWCP
protocol.null: permutation: permute gene->node labels on the graph (destroys biology, preserves degree), target perm p<0.01 for PGW-beats-LWCP

prereg_falsifiers: F1: PGW does NOT beat LWCP on conditional-coverage gap (perm p>=0.01) -> biology graph adds nothing beyond generic leverage -> NOT a novel primitive -> stays ~77
F2: isotonic sigma on graph-distance does NOT beat isotonic sigma on predictor feature-space difficulty -> reduces to standard normalized CP
F3: permuting gene identities on graph (preserving degree) does NOT degrade PGW -> graph carries no biological signal, only topology stats

tier_rule.clears_80: PGW beats BOTH vanilla AND LWCP on conditional-coverage gap with perm p<0.01, AND F3 fires (real permutation degrades it) -> genuinely novel validated primitive -> 80-83
tier_rule.stays_77: any falsifier F1/F2/F3 fires -> honest conclusion: rigorous application of known locally-adaptive CP, NOT a novel primitive -> 76-79
